speaker: Man
en: I have in this studio with me Mary Smith from York Town Tourism, who is here to tell us about some of the events happening in our state capital over the next three weeks at the Spring Festival.

speaker: Man
en: So, Mary, what can we expect to see?

speaker: Mary
en: Well, being in York Town is such an exciting time.

speaker: Mary
en: To kick off the Spring Festival, a vast firework will be displayed down by the lake, starting at 9 p.m. this Saturday, the 4th of September.

speaker: Mary
en: Over ten thousand fireworks will be set off.

speaker: Mary
en: All are choreographed to music and broadcast simultaneously here on Radio Yorkee.

speaker: Mary
en: You should get there early if you want to get close to the action.

speaker: Mary
en: So, bring along a picnic and a blanket, as it could get chilly in the evening.

speaker: Mary
en: One thing that attracts visitors to the festival from all over the country is the fantastic collection of flowers on show in Central Park throughout the festival.

speaker: Mary
en: Special buses will run daily from the town centre to the show at 20-minute intervals for those who prefer public transport.

speaker: Mary
en: If you are interested in seeing the latest in cars, from the fastest to the most expensive, then head over to The Motor Show at the Exhibition Centre from the 10th to the 15th of September.

speaker: Mary
en: It will be open daily from 9 a.m. until 10 p.m.

speaker: Mary
en: So, you can even pop in there after work.

speaker: Mary
en: Do you like photography?

speaker: Mary
en: Then go along to 'Grow Your Imagination', an exhibition of photographs of famous gardens, which will be held at the art gallery from the 11th to the 19th of September.

speaker: Mary
en: Come and be inspired by some of the world's most beautiful gardens.

speaker: Mary
en: I have a sneak preview of some of the photographs, and they are magnificent.

speaker: Mary
en: If music is more your thing, then you should come and hear the Australian Philharmonic Orchestra performing 'Swing in Spring' at the concert hall on Friday the 17th and Saturday the 18th of September.

speaker: Mary
en: It's a celebration of dance music from the 1940s and 50s.

speaker: Mary
en: There will be three performances.

speaker: Mary
en: Both evenings start at 7 p.m., and a matinee performance at 2.30 on Saturday.

speaker: Mary
en: So, get your dancing shoes on and hit there.

speaker: Mary
en: It's a guarantee to get your feet tapping.

speaker: Mary
en: Those are just a few of the attractions on offer.

speaker: Mary
en: But for something a little different, you could try 'Balloons Down Under', which is the largest gathering of hot air balloons in the southern hemisphere.

speaker: Mary
en: It's well worth it because over 25 balloons of all shapes and sizes will be a fantastic sight.

speaker: Mary
en: I'm also happy to announce that one lucky person will get to go up in one of these free balloons.

speaker: Mary
en: That is the prize in our unique Spring Festival competition.

speaker: Mary
en: It usually costs you 200 dollars.

speaker: Mary
en: So, it's not a bad prize.

speaker: Mary
en: I'm sure you all want a chance to win, so you'll need to fill out the entry form in today's paper of the York Town News.

speaker: Mary
en: Don't forget to include your phone number and send it to Radio Yorkee.

speaker: Mary
en: Make sure your entry reaches us by 5 p.m. on Thursday, the 9th of September.

speaker: Mary
en: Then to see if you've won, check out the festival's website on Saturday, the 11th of September, where we will publish the name of the lucky winner.

speaker: Mary
en: It's such a fantastic prize.

speaker: Mary
en: So, hurry up and get your entry in.

speaker: Mary
en: So there you have it, just a few special events happening here in York Town over the Spring Festival.

speaker: Mary
en: And if you like any more details about ...
